note: SYNTHESIZED from docs/research/spreset-behavior-2026-07-17.md §RegexBinding (clean-room; no SPreset source read). Prose is RPT-authored + scrambled; no ST default utility strings. Pins that regex stored INSIDE the preset under `extensions.SPreset.RegexBinding.regexes[]` (core-ST-shaped, placement 1 = user input) is INSTALLED and APPLIED — unblocking a preset whose only regex lives here. RPT installs it as preset-scoped regex tagged `origin:'spreset'` (kept DISTINCT from core `regex_scripts`) and, because RegexBinding is active, selects the `preset-first` regex tier order (spec default [2,0,1] = preset → global → character) — an explicit ordering MODE, not the upstream Object.values monkeypatch. Here the bound rule rewrites `kithara` → `lyre` on the user turn. Structural-only until the conformance adapter is wired (rptAdapter still returns null); the preset-first ORDER and the merge itself are pinned by unit tests (test/regexOrder.test.ts, test/spreset.test.ts).

input.description: Preset carrying a single SPreset RegexBinding rule (placement 1) that rewrites a token on the user turn; one system prompt; one user message.

character.name: Vael
character.description: Vael, keeper of the tide-glass at Sill.
